expected_findings.description: db.ts lacks parameterized query support — query() and transaction() only accept raw SQL strings, forcing all callers to concatenate user input. This is the systemic root cause of SQL injection across the codebase.
SQL injection in users.ts getUser(): userId is interpolated directly into a SELECT query string
Sensitive data exposure in users.ts getUser(): returns all fields including password_hash and api_key
SQL injection in users.ts searchUsers(): searchTerm is interpolated into LIKE clause without parameterization or escaping of SQL wildcards
SQL injection in users.ts updateUserRole(): userId and newRole interpolated into transaction queries
Timing side-channel in users.ts verifyPassword(): uses == for password comparison instead of constant-time compare
Path traversal in uploads.ts saveUpload(): user-controlled filename passed to path.join without validating the resolved path stays within the upload directory
No file size limit in uploads.ts saveUpload(): arbitrarily large uploads accepted
Path traversal in uploads.ts saveUploadExclusive(): same path.join vulnerability as saveUpload, user-controlled filename can escape upload directory
TOCTOU race condition in uploads.ts saveUploadExclusive(): gap between existsSync check and writeFileSync allows race condition
Insecure session token generation in session.ts: uses Math.random() which is not cryptographically secure
Session born expired in session.ts createSession(): expiry computed as Date.now() minus 24 hours instead of plus
SQL injection in session.ts createSession(): userId and token interpolated into audit log INSERT query
Synchronous session cleanup in session.ts: iterates entire session map synchronously, blocking the event loop
